summary: Tested PIN visibility restriction feature in User Configuration. Found and fixed a bug where users could not edit their own PIN due to incorrect field name comparison (user_id vs id). All 6 features now working correctly.
passed_tests: Admin (PIN 10000) can see and edit PINs of any user
Admin has eye button to show/hide PIN (lucide-eye icon)
Cajero (PIN 4321) editing other user (Carlos) sees 'Solo Admin' badge
Cajero editing other user sees asterisks '••••••••' instead of PIN field
Cajero editing other user sees '(Contacte al administrador)' message
Cajero editing his OWN profile CAN change his PIN (after fix)
critical_code_review_comments: BUG FOUND AND FIXED: UserConfig.js line 206 was comparing currentUser.user_id (undefined) with userId. The user object uses 'id' not 'user_id'. Fixed to currentUser.id
Code at lines 199-211: canEditPin logic correctly checks isAdmin || isEditingSelf || isNew
Code at lines 870-920: UI correctly shows restricted view (asterisks + badge + message) when canEditPin is false
Code at lines 895-903: Eye toggle button is correctly shown only for Admin users

bug_found_and_fixed.root_cause: The user object from login response uses 'id' field, not 'user_id'. The code was comparing undefined (currentUser.user_id) with the userId from URL params, causing isEditingSelf to always be false.
bug_found_and_fixed.impact: Users (non-admin) could not edit their own PIN even though the feature was intended to allow this

features_verified.admin_can_edit_any_pin: PASS - Admin sees PIN input with eye toggle button for all users
features_verified.admin_eye_button: PASS - Eye button (lucide-eye/lucide-eye-off) visible for Admin only
features_verified.cajero_cannot_edit_other_pin: PASS - Shows 'Solo Admin' badge + asterisks + 'Contacte al administrador'
features_verified.cajero_can_edit_own_pin: PASS - No restrictions when editing own profile (after fix)
features_verified.asterisks_shown: PASS - Non-admin users see '••••••••' instead of PIN input for other users
features_verified.solo_admin_badge: PASS - Badge with Lock icon appears next to PIN label for restricted users

seed_data_creation: None - used existing users (Admin, Carlos, Luis)
context_for_next_testing_agent: PIN restriction feature fully tested and fixed. The bug was in UserConfig.js line 206 where currentUser.user_id was used instead of currentUser.id. All permission checks now work correctly: Admin can edit all PINs, users can edit their own PIN, users cannot edit other users' PINs.

mocked_apis.note: No mocked APIs - all endpoints are real

rca_of_the_issue.description: The isEditingSelf check was comparing currentUser.user_id (which doesn't exist on the user object) with userId from URL params
reproduction_steps: 1. Login as non-admin user (e.g., Luis with PIN 4321)
3. Observe PIN field shows 'Solo Admin' restriction even for own profile
rca_of_the_issue.fix_applied: Changed currentUser?.user_id to currentUser?.id in line 206
rca_of_the_issue.verification: After fix, Luis can edit his own PIN but still cannot edit Carlos's PIN
